verses.latin_vulgate: Et ingressus perambulabat Jericho.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰσελθὼν διήρχετο τὴν ἰεριχώ
verses.king_james: And *Jesus* entered and passed through Jericho.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce vir nomine Zachæus: et hic princeps erat publicanorum, et ipse dives:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, ἀνὴρ ὀνόματι καλούμενος ξακχαῖος καὶ αὐτὸς ἦν ἀρχιτελώνης καὶ οὗτος ἦν πλούσιος
verses.king_james: And, behold, *there was* a man named Zacchaeus, which was the chief among the publicans, and he was rich.

verses.latin_vulgate: et quærebat videre Jesum, quis esset: et non poterat præ turba, quia statura pusillus erat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐζήτει ἰδεῖν τὸν ἰησοῦν τίς ἐστιν καὶ οὐκ ἠδύνατο ἀπὸ τοῦ ὄχλου ὅτι τῇ ἡλικίᾳ μικρὸς ἦν
verses.king_james: And he sought to see Jesus who he was; and could not for the press, because he was little of stature.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et præcurrens ascendit in arborem sycomorum ut videret eum: quia inde erat transiturus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προδραμὼν ἔμπροσθεν ἀνέβη ἐπὶ συκομωραίαν, ἵνα ἴδῃ αὐτόν ὅτι δι᾽ ἐκείνης ἤμελλεν διέρχεσθαι
verses.king_james: And he ran before, and climbed up into a sycomore tree to see him: for he was to pass that *way*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum venisset ad locum, suspiciens Jesus vidit illum, et dixit ad eum: Zachæe, festinans descende: quia hodie in domo tua oportet me manere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὡς ἦλθεν ἐπὶ τὸν τόπον ἀναβλέψας ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶδεν αὐτόν καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτόν, ξακχαῖε σπεύσας κατάβηθι σήμερον γὰρ ἐν τῷ οἴκῳ σου δεῖ με μεῖναι
verses.king_james: And when Jesus came to the place, he looked up, and saw him, and said unto him, Zacchaeus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et festinans descendit, et excepit illum gaudens.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ σπεύσας κατέβη καὶ ὑπεδέξατο αὐτὸν χαίρων
verses.king_james: And he made haste, and came down, and received him joyfully.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum viderent omnes, murmurabant, dicentes quod ad hominem peccatorem divertisset.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδόντες ἅπαντες διεγόγγυζον λέγοντες ὅτι παρὰ ἁμαρτωλῷ ἀνδρὶ εἰσῆλθεν καταλῦσαι
verses.king_james: And when they saw *it*, they all murmured, saying, That he was gone to be guest with a man that is a sinner.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stans autem Zachæus, dixit ad Dominum: Ecce dimidium bonorum meorum, Domine, do pauperibus: et si quid aliquem defraudavi, reddo quadruplum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σταθεὶς δὲ ξακχαῖος εἶπεν πρὸς τὸν κύριον ἰδού, τὰ ἡμίση τῶν ὑπαρχόντων μου κύριε δίδωμι τοῖς πτωχοῖς καὶ εἴ τινός τι ἐσυκοφάντησα ἀποδίδωμι τετραπλοῦν
verses.king_james: And Zacchaeus stood, and said unto the Lord; Behold, Lord, the half of my goods I give to the poor; and if I have taken any thing from any man by false accusation, I restore *him* fourfold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait Jesus ad eum: Quia hodie salus domui huic facta est: eo quod et ipse filius sit Abrahæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς αὐτὸν ὁ ἰησοῦς ὅτι σήμερον σωτηρία τῷ οἴκῳ τούτῳ ἐγένετο καθότι καὶ αὐτὸς υἱὸς ἀβραάμ ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto him, This day is salvation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit enim Filius hominis quærere, et salvum facere quod perierat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦλθεν γὰρ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ζητῆσαι καὶ σῶσαι τὸ ἀπολωλός
verses.king_james: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc illis audientibus adjiciens, dixit parabolam, eo quod esset prope Jerusalem: et quia existimarent quod confestim regnum Dei manifestaretur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκουόντων δὲ αὐτῶν ταῦτα προσθεὶς εἶπεν παραβολὴν διὰ τὸ ἐγγὺς αὐτὸν εἶναι ἰερουσαλὴμ καὶ δοκεῖν αὐτοὺς ὅτι παραχρῆμα μέλλει ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ ἀναφαίνεσθαι
verses.king_james: And as they heard these things, he added and spake a parable, because he was nigh to Jerusalem, and because they thought that the kingdom of God should immediately appear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo: Homo quidam nobilis abiit in regionem longinquam accipere sibi regnum, et reverti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν ἄνθρωπός τις εὐγενὴς ἐπορεύθη εἰς χώραν μακρὰν λαβεῖν ἑαυτῷ βασιλείαν καὶ ὑποστρέψαι
verses.king_james: He said therefore, A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocatis autem decem servis suis, dedit eis decem mnas, et ait ad illos: Negotiamini dum venio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καλέσας δὲ δέκα δούλους ἑαυτοῦ ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς δέκα μνᾶς καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς πραγματεύσασθε ἕως ἔρχομαι
verses.king_james: And he called his ten servants, and delivered them ten pounds, and said unto them, Occupy till I come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cives autem ejus oderant eum: et miserunt legationem post illum, dicentes: Nolumus hunc regnare super nos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ πολῖται αὐτοῦ ἐμίσουν αὐτόν καὶ ἀπέστειλαν πρεσβείαν ὀπίσω αὐτοῦ λέγοντες οὐ θέλομεν τοῦτον βασιλεῦσαι ἐφ᾽ ἡμᾶς
verses.king_james: But his citizens hated him, and sent a message after him, saying, We will not have this *man* to reign over us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est ut rediret accepto regno: et jussit vocari servos, quibus dedit pecuniam, ut sciret quantum quisque negotiatus esset.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐν τῷ ἐπανελθεῖν αὐτὸν λαβόντα τὴν βασιλείαν καὶ εἶπεν φωνηθῆναι αὐτῷ τοὺς δούλους τούτους οἷς ἔδωκεν τὸ ἀργύριον ἵνα γνῷ τίς τί διεπραγματεύσατο
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that when he was returned, having received the kingdom, then he commanded these servants to be called unto him, to whom he had given the money, that he might know how much every man had gained by trading.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit autem primus dicens: Domine, mna tua decem mnas acquisivit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρεγένετο δὲ ὁ πρῶτος λέγων, κύριε ἡ μνᾶ σου προσειργάσατο δέκα μνᾶς
verses.king_james: Then came the first, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained ten pounds.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illi: Euge bone serve, quia in modico fuisti fidelis, eris potestatem habens super decem civitates.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ εὖ, ἀγαθὲ δοῦλε ὅτι ἐν ἐλαχίστῳ πιστὸς ἐγένου ἴσθι ἐξουσίαν ἔχων ἐπάνω δέκα πόλεων
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, Well, thou good servant: because thou hast been faithful in a very little, have thou authority over ten cities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et alter venit, dicens: Domine, mna tua fecit quinque mnas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦλθεν ὁ δεύτερος λέγων, κύριε ἡ μνᾶ σου ἐποίησεν πέντε μνᾶς
verses.king_james: And the second came, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained five pounds.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et huic ait: Et tu esto super quinque civitates.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ καὶ τούτῳ καὶ σὺ γίνου ἐπάνω πέντε πόλεων
verses.king_james: And he said likewise to him, Be thou also over five cities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et alter venit, dicens: Domine, ecce mna tua, quam habui repositam in sudario:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἕτερος ἦλθεν λέγων, κύριε ἰδού, ἡ μνᾶ σου ἣν εἶχον ἀποκειμένην ἐν σουδαρίῳ
verses.king_james: And another came, saying, Lord, behold, *here is* thy pound, which I have kept laid up in a napkin:

verses.latin_vulgate: timui enim te, quia homo austerus es: tollis quod non posuisti, et metis quod non seminasti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐφοβούμην γάρ σε ὅτι ἄνθρωπος αὐστηρὸς εἶ αἴρεις ὃ οὐκ ἔθηκας καὶ θερίζεις ὃ οὐκ ἔσπειρας
verses.king_james: For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou layedst not down, and reapest that thou didst not sow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei: De ore tuo te judico, serve nequam. Sciebas quod ego homo austerus sum, tollens quod non posui, et metens quod non seminavi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει δὲ αὐτῷ ἐκ τοῦ στόματός σου κρινῶ σε πονηρὲ δοῦλε ᾔδεις ὅτι ἐγὼ ἄνθρωπος αὐστηρός εἰμι αἴρων ὃ οὐκ ἔθηκα καὶ θερίζων ὃ οὐκ ἔσπειρα
verses.king_james: And he saith unto him, Out of thine own mouth will I judge thee, *thou* wicked servant. Thou knewest that I was an austere man, taking up that I laid not down, and reaping that I did not sow:

verses.latin_vulgate: et quare non dedisti pecuniam meam ad mensam, ut ego veniens cum usuris utique exegissem illam?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διατί οὐκ ἔδωκάς τὸ ἀργύριον μου ἐπὶ τὴν τράπεζαν καὶ ἐγὼ ἐλθὼν σὺν τόκῳ ἂν ἔπραξα αὐτὸ
verses.king_james: Wherefore then gavest not thou my money into the bank, that at my coming I might have required mine own with usury?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et astantibus dixit: Auferte ab illo mnam, et date illi qui decem mnas habet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τοῖς παρεστῶσιν εἶπεν ἄρατε ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ τὴν μνᾶν καὶ δότε τῷ τὰς δέκα μνᾶς ἔχοντι
verses.king_james: And he said unto them that stood by, Take from him the pound, and give *it* to him that hath ten pounds.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixerunt ei: Domine, habet decem mnas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ κύριε ἔχει δέκα μνᾶς
verses.king_james: (And they said unto him, Lord, he hath ten pounds.)

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico autem vobis, quia omni habenti dabitur, et abundabit: ab eo autem qui non habet, et quod habet auferetur ab eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω γὰρ ὑμῖν ὅτι παντὶ τῷ ἔχοντι δοθήσεται ἀπὸ δὲ τοῦ μὴ ἔχοντος καὶ ὃ ἔχει ἀρθήσεται ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For I say unto you, That unto every one which hath shall be given; and from him that hath not, even that he hath shall be taken away from him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen inimicos meos illos, qui noluerunt me regnare super se, adducite huc: et interficite ante me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πλὴν τοὺς ἐχθρούς μου ἐκείνους, τοὺς μὴ θελήσαντάς με βασιλεῦσαι ἐπ᾽ αὐτοὺς ἀγάγετε ὧδε καὶ κατασφάξατε ἔμπροσθέν μου
verses.king_james: But those mine enemies, which would not that I should reign over them, bring hither, and slay *them* before me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et his dictis, præcedebat ascendens Jerosolymam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰπὼν ταῦτα ἐπορεύετο ἔμπροσθεν ἀναβαίνων εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα
verses.king_james: And when he had thus spoken, he went before, ascending up to Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est, cum appropinquasset ad Bethphage et Bethaniam, ad montem qui vocatur Oliveti, misit duos discipulos suos,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ὡς ἤγγισεν εἰς βηθφαγὴ καὶ βηθανίαν πρὸς τὸ ὄρος τὸ καλούμενον ἐλαιῶν ἀπέστειλεν δύο τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ,
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when he was come nigh to Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount called *the mount* of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens: Ite in castellum quod contra est: in quod introëuntes, invenietis pullum asinæ alligatum, cui nemo umquam hominum sedit: solvite illum, et adducite.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰπὼν, ὑπάγετε εἰς τὴν κατέναντι κώμην ἐν ᾗ εἰσπορευόμενοι εὑρήσετε πῶλον δεδεμένον ἐφ᾽ ὃν οὐδεὶς πώποτε ἀνθρώπων ἐκάθισεν λύσαντες αὐτὸν ἀγάγετε
verses.king_james: Saying, Go ye into the village over against *you*; in the which at your entering ye shall find a colt tied, whereon yet never man sat: loose him, and bring *him hither*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si quis vos interrogaverit: Quare solvitis? sic dicetis ei: Quia Dominus operam ejus desiderat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐάν τις ὑμᾶς ἐρωτᾷ διατί λύετε οὕτως ἐρεῖτε αὐτῷ, ὅτι ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ χρείαν ἔχει
verses.king_james: And if any man ask you, Why do ye loose *him* ? thus shall ye say unto him, Because the Lord hath need of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abierunt autem qui missi erant: et invenerunt, sicut dixit illis, stantem pullum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπελθόντες δὲ οἱ ἀπεσταλμένοι εὗρον καθὼς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And they that were sent went their way, and found even as he had said unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Solventibus autem illis pullum, dixerunt domini ejus ad illos: Quid solvitis pullum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λυόντων δὲ αὐτῶν τὸν πῶλον εἶπον οἱ κύριοι αὐτοῦ πρὸς αὐτούς τί λύετε τὸν πῶλον
verses.king_james: And as they were loosing the colt, the owners thereof said unto them, Why loose ye the colt?

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi dixerunt: Quia Dominus eum necessarium habet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ εἶπον ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ χρείαν ἔχει
verses.king_james: And they said, The Lord hath need of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et duxerunt illum ad Jesum. Et jacentes vestimenta sua supra pullum, imposuerunt Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤγαγον αὐτὸν πρὸς τὸν ἰησοῦν καὶ ἐπιῤῥίψαντες ἑαυτῶν τὰ ἱμάτια ἐπὶ τὸν πῶλον ἐπεβίβασαν τὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: And they brought him to Jesus: and they cast their garments upon the colt, and they set Jesus thereon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eunte autem illo, substernebant vestimenta sua in via:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πορευομένου δὲ αὐτοῦ ὑπεστρώννυον τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτῶν ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ
verses.king_james: And as he went, they spread their clothes in the way.

verses.latin_vulgate: et cum appropinquaret jam ad descensum montis Oliveti, cœperunt omnes turbæ discipulorum gaudentes laudare Deum voce magna super omnibus, quas viderant, virtutibus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγγίζοντος δὲ αὐτοῦ ἤδη πρὸς τῇ καταβάσει τοῦ ὄρους τῶν ἐλαιῶν ἤρξαντο ἅπαν τὸ πλῆθος τῶν μαθητῶν χαίροντες αἰνεῖν τὸν θεὸν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ περὶ πασῶν ὧν εἶδον δυνάμεων
verses.king_james: And when he was come nigh, even now at the descent of the mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works that they had seen;

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes: Benedictus, qui venit rex in nomine Domini: pax in cælo, et gloria in excelsis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντες εὐλογημένος ὁ ἐρχόμενος βασιλεὺς ἐν ὀνόματι κυρίου εἰρήνη ἐν οὐρανῷ καὶ δόξα ἐν ὑψίστοις
verses.king_james: Saying, Blessed *be* the King that cometh in the name of the Lord: peace in heaven, and glory in the highest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quidam pharisæorum de turbis dixerunt ad illum: Magister, increpa discipulos tuos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καί τινες τῶν φαρισαίων ἀπὸ τοῦ ὄχλου εἶπον πρὸς αὐτόν διδάσκαλε ἐπιτίμησον τοῖς μαθηταῖς σου
verses.king_james: And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude said unto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus ipse ait: Dico vobis, quia si hi tacuerint, lapides clamabunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς, λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι, ἐὰν οὗτοι σιωπήσωσιν, οἱ λίθοι κεκράξονται
verses.king_james: And he answered and said unto them, I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ut appropinquavit, videns civitatem flevit super illam, dicens:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὡς ἤγγισεν ἰδὼν τὴν πόλιν ἔκλαυσεν ἐπ᾽ αὐτή,
verses.king_james: And when he was come near, he beheld the city, and wept over it,

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia si cognovisses et tu, et quidem in hac die tua, quæ ad pacem tibi: nunc autem abscondita sunt ab oculis tuis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγων ὅτι εἰ ἔγνως καὶ σὺ καὶ γε ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ σου ταύτῃ τὰ πρὸς εἰρήνην σου νῦν δὲ ἐκρύβη ἀπὸ ὀφθαλμῶν σου
verses.king_james: Saying, If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things *which belong* unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia venient dies in te: et circumdabunt te inimici tui vallo, et circumdabunt te: et coangustabunt te undique:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἥξουσιν ἡμέραι ἐπὶ σὲ καὶ περιβαλοῦσιν οἱ ἐχθροί σου χάρακά σοι καὶ περικυκλώσουσίν σε καὶ συνέξουσίν σε πάντοθεν
verses.king_james: For the days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side,

verses.latin_vulgate: et ad terram prosternent te, et filios tuos, qui in te sunt, et non relinquent in te lapidem super lapidem: eo quod non cognoveris tempus visitationis tuæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐδαφιοῦσίν σε καὶ τὰ τέκνα σου ἐν σοί καὶ οὐκ ἀφήσουσιν ἐν σοί λίθον ἐπὶ λίθῳ ἀνθ ὧν οὐκ ἔγνως τὸν καιρὸν τῆς ἐπισκοπῆς σου
verses.king_james: And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ingressus in templum, cœpit ejicere vendentes in illo, et ementes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰσελθὼν εἰς τὸ ἱερὸν ἤρξατο ἐκβάλλειν τοὺς πωλοῦντας ἐν αὐτῷ καὶ ἀγοράζοντας,
verses.king_james: And he went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold therein, and them that bought;

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens illis: Scriptum est: Quia domus mea domus orationis est: vos autem fecistis illam speluncam latronum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγων αὐτοῖς, γέγραπται ὁ οἰκός μου οἰκος προσευχῆς ἐστίν, ὑμεῖς δὲ αὐτὸν ἐποιήσατε σπήλαιον λῃστῶν
verses.king_james: Saying unto them, It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of thieves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat docens quotidie in templo. Principes autem sacerdotum, et scribæ, et princeps plebis quærebant illum perdere:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν διδάσκων τὸ καθ᾽ ἡμέραν ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ οἱ δὲ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς ἐζήτουν αὐτὸν ἀπολέσαι καὶ οἱ πρῶτοι τοῦ λαοῦ
verses.king_james: And he taught daily in the temple. But the chief priests and the scribes and the chief of the people sought to destroy him,

verses.latin_vulgate: et non inveniebant quid facerent illi. Omnis enim populus suspensus erat, audiens illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐχ εὕρισκον τὸ τί ποιήσωσιν ὁ λαὸς γὰρ ἅπας ἐξεκρέματο αὐτοῦ ἀκούων
verses.king_james: And could not find what they might do: for all the people were very attentive to hear him.
